page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 15 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 7.5 GB per vCPU. Powered by an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8581C processor operating at 2.3 GHz, this instance allocates 1 physical core with 2 threads. The CPU features a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache, alongside advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for specialized vector and matrix operations.

Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance delivers strong single-core performance, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 2229.0 and strong single-threaded compression and decompression rates, such as 34.51 GB/sec in gzip. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are comparatively limited. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance is in the bottom 10%, and uncached memory read and write bandwidths measure 18.79 GB/sec and 12.28 GB/sec respectively.

This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highmem-2 offers a dense memory allocation per vCPU for workloads that require moderate memory footprints but do not demand high multi-threaded throughput or massive memory bandwidth. It is best suited for single-threaded applications, text processing, light database operations, and low-concurrency web serving.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance configured with 2 vCPUs and 15 GB of RAM, providing 7.5 GB of memory per core. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it utilizes a dedicated Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz with 1 physical core and 2 threads. While the instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, it features advanced CPU instructions like Intel AMX and AVX-512. Benchmark results highlight strong single-core performance, including single-threaded gzip compression and text processing. However, multi-core execution and uncached memory bandwidth are relatively weak. This profile makes the c4-highmem-2 qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-dependent, single-threaded workloads, text processing, and lightweight database operations rather than highly parallelized or memory-bandwidth-intensive tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-2 is a memory-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 15 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C CPU, delivering strong single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-2 is an x86_64 instance with 2 vCPUs and 15 GB RAM, delivering strong single-core performance for memory-focused tasks.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring strong single-core performance and advanced matrix extensions for specialized, low-concurrency workloads.
bullet_points: Equipped with 2 vCPUs and 15 GB of RAM on a dedicated Intel Xeon 8581C processor
Features Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and AVX-512 instruction sets
Delivers strong single-core performance and single-threaded compression
Lacks local storage and GPU acceleration, relying entirely on network-attached resources
Best suited for single-threaded applications, text processing, and light database workloads
